Add necessary columns to the table.
The question asks about the number of times he competed in Australia. We need to know the country of each competition venue. We extract the value from column "venue" and create a different column "country" for each row. The datatype is String.

Select relevant rows.
The question aims to find out how many times the athlete competed in Australia. We need to look at the relevant rows in the table that mention Australia as the venue, which are row 1 (Sydney, Australia) and row 6 (Melbourne, Australia) to get the answer.

Filter out useless columns.

The question is asking to count how many times the athlete competed in Australia. Since each row represents a competition and the "venue" column indicates the location of each competition, we need to group by the "venue" column to check how many times Australia appears in the venues. We can use the f_group_column(venue) operation to group the competitions based on their venue to analyze this situation.
The athlete competed in Australia twice: once in 1996 at the World Junior Championships in Sydney, and again in 2001 at the IAAF Grand Prix Final in Melbourne.
